---
title: "LRPITT14b: Unadjusted modified-ITT offer effect on the SES complete-case subset, letter sounds"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT14B is the **unadjusted available-case modified-ITT** analysis for letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK) (`yarclet`) on the **SES complete-case subset**—the matched comparator to LRPITT13b. It adjusts for no SES covariates but deliberately restricts to the same rows (`restrict_complete`): LRPITT13b versus LRPITT14B isolates adjustment with the selected sample fixed, while comparison with LRPITT07 isolates the additional complete-case selection. The estimand is the randomised offer effect on $L_{\text{post}}$ in that SES-complete fitted sample, not a full-population effect for all 57 randomised children.

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so randomisation identifies the offer contrast $\tau$ in the SES-complete fitted sample without adjustment. Extending it beyond that selected observed-data sample requires ignorable exclusion and missingness. The own baseline and linear age are
**precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are not what licenses the causal
interpretation. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a
**positive** $\tau$ means the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $L_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 32$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
No covariate adjustment; the fit is restricted to the SES complete-case rows. HSGPs are off by default.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
